fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The type of reflection algebras and homomorphisms

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We will write { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } : \equiv \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } } {A \to X} for the type of reflection algebras under A; given two reflection algebras X,Y: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } we will write { \mathopen {} \left ( X \multimap Y \right ) \mathclose {}} : \equiv \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } } { \eta _{ Y } } \sim _{ A \to Y } { f \circ \eta _{ X } } for the type of homomorphisms of reflection algebras.

Let \mathcal {U} be a subuniverse of \mathcal {V} and let A: \mathcal {V} be a type. A reflection algebra X under A in \mathcal {U} is defined to be a type UX: \mathcal {U} together with a function { A } \xrightarrow {{ \eta _{ X } }}{ UX }. A homomorphism f of reflection algebras from X to Y is given by a function { UX } \xrightarrow {{ Uf }}{ UY } together with a homotopy \eta _{ f } : { \eta _{ Y } } \sim _{ A \to Y } { Uf \circ \eta _X }.
When it causes no ambiguity, we will write X: \mathcal {U} for the underlying type UX of a given reflection algebra X; likewise, we will write f x rather than Uf x.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: The type of reflection algebras and homomorphisms

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We will write { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } : \equiv \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( X: \mathcal {U} \right ) \mathclose {}} } } {A \to X} for the type of reflection algebras under A; given two reflection algebras X,Y: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } we will write { \mathopen {} \left ( X \multimap Y \right ) \mathclose {}} : \equiv \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( f:X \to Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } } { \eta _{ Y } } \sim _{ A \to Y } { f \circ \eta _{ X } } for the type of homomorphisms of reflection algebras.

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Identity and composition of reflection algebra homomorphisms

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let X: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } be a reflection algebra; we define the identity homomorphism 1 _{ X } :X \multimap X on X as follows:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {array}{l} 1 _{ X } x : \equiv x \\ \eta _{ 1 _{ X } } a : \equiv \mathsf { refl } \end {array}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Now let Y,Z: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } be two further reflection algebras and let f : X \multimap Y and g : Y \multimap Z be two homomorphisms of reflection algebras. The composite homomorphism f;g : X \multimap Z is defined like so:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {array}{l} { \mathopen {} \left ( f;g \right ) \mathclose {}} x : \equiv g { \mathopen {} \left ( fx \right ) \mathclose {}} \\ \eta _{ f;g } a : \equiv \mathsf { ap } _{ g } \, { \mathopen {} \left ( \eta _{ f } a \right ) \mathclose {}} \bullet \eta _{ g } a \end {array}

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Given a pair of reflection algebras X,Y: { \mathcal {U} } \downarrow { A } and a pair of homomorphisms f,g : X \multimap Y, a homotopy from f to g is defined to be a homotopy \phi : { Uf } \sim _{ X \to Y } { Ug } between the underlying functions together with a witness \bar { \phi } of the following coherence:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \bar \phi : \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } } { \eta _{ g } a } =_{ { g { \mathopen {} \left ( \eta _{ X } a \right ) \mathclose {}} } =_{ Y } { \eta _{ Y } a } } { \eta _{ f } a \bullet \phi { \mathopen {} \left ( \eta _{ X } a \right ) \mathclose {}} }
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: We shall write { f } \sim _{ X \multimap Y } { g } for the type of homotopies from the homomorphism f to the homomorphism g.

Given a path of reflection algebras X,Y: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } and a homomorphism f : X \multimap Y, the type \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( g:X \multimap Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( g:X \multimap Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( g:X \multimap Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( g:X \multimap Y \right ) \mathclose {}} } } { { g } \sim _{ X \multimap Y } { f } } of homomorphisms equipped with a homotopy to f is contractible, by function extensionality and the characterization of path spaces in dependent sums. Hence it follows that the canonical map { f } =_{ X \multimap Y } { g } \to { f } \sim _{ X \multimap Y } { g } is an equivalence.

Given a type K and a family of reflection algebras X : K \to { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } such that the dependent product \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } } U { \mathopen {} \left ( Xk \right ) \mathclose {}} lies in \mathcal {U}, we may define the product of product of reflection algebras \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } } Xk to have the following structure map:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {array}{l} \eta _{ \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } } Xk } : A \to \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } } {Xk} \\ \eta _{ \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( k:K \right ) \mathclose {}} } } Xk } a : \equiv \lambda k:K. \, \eta _{ Xk } a \end {array}

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: Let X: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } be a reflection algebra; a displayed reflection algebra Y over X is defined to be a dependent type \bar {U}Y : X \to \mathcal {U} together with a dependent function \bar { \eta } _{ Y } : \mathchoice { \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \prod _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( a:A \right ) \mathclose {}} } } { \bar {U}Y { \mathopen {} \left ( \eta _{ X } a \right ) \mathclose {}} }.

Given a displayed reflection algebra Y over a reflection algebra X: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} }, we may define the total reflection algebra \widetilde {Y}: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} } of Y as follows:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {array}{l} U \widetilde {Y} : \equiv \mathchoice { \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( x:Ux \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \textstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( x:Ux \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( x:Ux \right ) \mathclose {}} } }{ \scriptscriptstyle \sum _{ { \mathopen {} \left ( x:Ux \right ) \mathclose {}} } } Y[x] \\ \eta _{ \widetilde {Y} } a : \equiv { \mathopen {} \left ( \eta _{ X } a, \bar { \eta } _{ Y } a \right ) \mathclose {}} \end {array}
fr:mainmatter.fr:p: The projection function { U \widetilde {Y} } \xrightarrow {{ \pi _1 }}{ UX } extends to a homomorphism of reflection algebras \pi _{ Y } : \widetilde {Y} \multimap X as follows:
fr:mainmatter.fr:tex: \begin {array}{l} \pi _{ Y } { \mathopen {} \left ( x,y \right ) \mathclose {}} : \equiv x \\ \alpha _{ \pi _{ Y } } a : \equiv \mathsf { refl } \end {array}

fr:frontmatter.fr:title: Displayed reflection algebras over displayed reflection algebras

fr:mainmatter.fr:p: If Y is a displayed reflection algebra over a reflection algebra X: { A } \downarrow { \mathcal {U} }, we define a displayed reflection algebra over Y to be a displayed reflection algebra in the original sense over the total reflection algebra \widetilde {Y} corresponding to Y.

Let \mathcal {U} be a subuniverse of \mathcal {V} and let A: \mathcal {V} be a type. A reflection algebra X under A in \mathcal {U} is defined to be a type UX: \mathcal {U} together with a function { A } \xrightarrow {{ \eta _{ X } }}{ UX }. A homomorphism f of reflection algebras from X to Y is given by a function { UX } \xrightarrow {{ Uf }}{ UY } together with a homotopy \eta _{ f } : { \eta _{ Y } } \sim _{ A \to Y } { Uf \circ \eta _X }.
When it causes no ambiguity, we will write X: \mathcal {U} for the underlying type UX of a given reflection algebra X; likewise, we will write f x rather than Uf x.
